title: [SQL] Optimize "constant views"
author: mihaibudiu
body:
For some types of tables it may be possible to implement them as constant views, e.g.:

```sql
CREATE VIEW const_view AS
WITH const(id, label) AS (
    VALUES
        (1, 'alpha'),
        (2, 'beta'),
        (3, 'gamma')
)
SELECT *
FROM const;
```

We already have a couple of optimizations for pushing these constants down in plans, but we could use more. In particular, a JOIN against a constant view could become a MAP operator.

title: [web-ui] Display a latency histogram in web ui
author: mihaibudiu
body: This would be useful to highlight the speed of the platform.

title: [question] DBSP crate - Windows support?
author: vpopescu
body:
HI,
What is the statement on windows support? I created a minimal build and it fails:

```sh
   Compiling hyper-util v0.1.20
   Compiling actix-web v4.14.0
   Compiling feldera-samply v0.319.0
error[E0432]: unresolved import `nix::time`
  --> C:\Users\val\.cargo\registry\src\index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f\feldera-samply-0.319.0\src\lib.rs:66:10
   |
66 | use nix::time::{ClockId, clock_gettime};
   |          ^^^^ could not find `time` in `nix`

error[E0433]: cannot find `unistd` in `nix`
   --> C:\Users\val\.cargo\registry\src\index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f\feldera-samply-0.319.0\src\lib.rs:716:29
    |
716 |                 .entry(nix::unistd::getpid().as_raw() as usize)
    |                             ^^^^^^ could not find `unistd` in `nix`

Some errors have detailed explanations: E0432, E0433.
```

is it worth for me to fix those issues, or am I going to be running into more issues down the line? It's an absolute minimal app:

```ini
[package]
name = "dbsp-probe"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"


[dependencies]
dbsp = { version = "0.319.0", default-features = false }
```

title: Push-based pipeline tracking endpoint
author: snkas
body:
Rather than the poll-based pipeline tracking we have currently, instead an endpoint where you can subscribe to changes to the pipeline. The server, while the connection to the client remains open, still does the polling to the database, but only pushes when changes occur. To do this efficiently, additional versioning is needed for the pipeline table.

An implementation of this is already present in this (currently closed) PR: https://github.com/feldera/feldera/pull/6088 . It can be reopened.

**Advantages:**
- Less network bandwidth usage
- Lower latency change propagation
- Addition of new (large) fields does not have to be separately handled

**Disadvantages:**
- More complex

This feature does require getting rid of the global Mutex around the database connection pool, such that multiple connections are used simultaneously by the API server. Otherwise, the increased polling rate of the database might lead to long response times if it can't keep up.

title: Incorrect handling of same-name tag in Web Console with two colors
author: snkas
body:
**Reproduction:**
1. Create a pipeline `example123`
2. Run:
   ```
   curl -i -X PATCH http://localhost:8080/v0/pipelines/example123 -d '{"tags": ["example|ff0000", "example|00ff00"]}' -H 'Content-Type: application/json'
   ```
3. Create another pipeline `example456`
4. Try add/remove the two possible `example` tags

title: [connectors] Delta follow/snapshot_and_follow re-ingests the whole file on a deletion-vector delete
author: swanandx
body:
In follow and snapshot_and_follow mode, a deletion-vector delete of K rows from an N-row data file makes the connector ingest ~2N−K records instead of K. The Remove action is read as a full-file retraction (all N rows) and the same-path Remove action is read as a full-file retraction and the re-added file (with the new DV) as ~N−K inserts; DBSP then cancels the unchanged majority. 

**_Result is correct_**, just wasted computation

**Minimal repro**: DV-enabled Delta table, one 100-row data file (v1). Delete a single row (v2, merge-on-read DV). Pipeline reads it in snapshot_and_follow from version 1.
- COUNT(*): 100 → 99 (correct, −1)
- records_input_total: 100 → 299 (+199 to delete 1 row)

**Expected**: the delete should ingest ≈ K records (the rows the DV newly masks), not ≈ 2N.

**Root cause**: process_log_entry (non-CDC branch) processes each Add/Remove independently over the full file; it does not recognize that a same-path Add+Remove pair differs only by deletion vector, so it never reads just the DV delta.

title: [profiler] Large circuit profiles fail to download
author: ryzhyk
body: We still have the problem with very large profiles (e.g., large circuit + 64 workers) getting dropped between pipeline and the manager (probably due to the body size limit). Let's figure out how to compress or stream them or at least increase the limit.

title: [profiler] The profiler shouldn't refuse to open bundles without a circuit profile
author: ryzhyk
body: Even if the bundle is missing the profile, it still has program code, config, logs, etc., all of which can be visualized by the profiler.

title: [connectors] Delta snapshot read fails on UC-Uniform-over-Iceberg tables
author: swanandx
body:
**Describe the bug**

Reading a Unity Catalog Uniform table (Iceberg files exposed as Delta, columnMapping.mode=id) fails the initial snapshot read:

```
error retrieving initial snapshot query 'select "after","op" from snapshot':
Execution("Non-nullable column 'col-102' is missing from the physical schema")
```

**Root cause**

Under mode=id, Delta refers to each column by physicalName = col-<field-id>. But data files written by a native Iceberg writer (Flink/pyiceberg) use logical names (op, after) + a Parquet field_id. delta-rs's "next" scan provider resolves columns by physical name (DefaultPhysicalExprAdapter), so col-102 is absent from every file -> missing -> hard error (non-nullable) or silent NULL-fill (nullable). The field_id that would resolve it is present on both sides but unused. No schema evolution involved.

**Confirmed (file inspection of a UC-Uniform/pyiceberg table)**
- Parquet: required binary field_id=8 op — logical name, nullable=false.
- UC-generated Delta log (engineInfo: Kernel-…/Iceberg REST Catalog) stats address it as col-8.
- Feldera -> Non-nullable column 'col-8' is missing from the physical schema. (field_id 8 → col-8 mirrors customer's 102 → col-102.)
- Databricks-engine (USING iceberg) tables write col-N on disk and are unaffected — the writer is the only variable.

**Impact**
- Non-nullable missing column -> hard read failure. Nullable -> silently NULL (data-correctness risk).
- Affects UC Uniform over Flink/pyiceberg/Spark-Iceberg. Native Delta + Databricks-managed Iceberg unaffected.

**Fix ( required in delta-rs, not in feldera )**

Resolve column-mapped reads by field-id when present, fall back to name (fork patch on delta-rs, worth upstreaming):
1. When column_mapping_mode == Id, propagate delta.columnMapping.id onto the Arrow read schema as PARQUET:field_id (currently dropped by try_into_arrow()).
2. Field-id-aware PhysicalExprAdapterFactory: match by PARQUET:field_id when both sides have it, else by name (delegates unchanged when no field-ids -> native Delta untouched).

*Repro*: native Iceberg writer (pyiceberg via UC Iceberg REST) + op NOT NULL; or zero-dep local fixture (hand-written _delta_log over logical-name Parquet) for regression.

title: [WebConsole] source code navigation is confusing
author: ryzhyk
body:
- Users don't know that they need to double-click to jump to code.
- Even with brighter highlighting, people still don't realize that the code window is showing the selected operator after a double click. Should it blink a few times?
- It gets worse with composite nodes, since it's impossible to figure out that the user must double-click to expand and _then_ double-click again on the node with a diamond.

Connector error messages can be quite long. They are impossible to read if the panel cannot be resized by moving the separator.
Same for the health panel.
